alt_text: Line graph of temperature during one day, read every two hours: 6 am 11°C, 8 am 13°C, 10 am 18°C, 12 noon 22°C, 2 pm 24°C, 4 pm 20°C, 6 pm 16°C. The vertical axis shows temperature from 0 to 30 degrees Celsius with gridlines every 5.

Column graph of monthly rainfall at Warrigal Creek Public School in millimetres: January 90, February 70, March 110, April 40, May 60, June 30. The vertical axis shows rainfall in mm from 0 to 120 with gridlines every 20.

A coordinate grid numbered 0 to 8 on both axes. A right-angled triangle has corners labelled P at (1, 1), Q at (1, 4) and R at (3, 1). The right angle is at P. A dashed arrow points up and to the right, indicating the triangle will be slid 4 units right and 2 units up.

A three-by-three grid of shapes. Each row runs circle, square, triangle from left to right; each column grows small, medium, large from top to bottom. The bottom-right cell is a dashed question-mark box. The missing shape is a large triangle.
A sequence of four squares, each holding one more dot than the last: 1 dot, 2 dots, 3 dots, then 4 dots, followed by a question-mark box. The next square in the pattern holds 5 dots.
